page: Google Cloud Platform ct5lp-hightpu-8t is a specialized accelerator-optimized server instance within the ct5lp family. It features 224 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 384 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of approximately 1.71 GB per vCPU, prioritizing raw compute and accelerator throughput over large system memory footprints.

The primary performance driver of this instance is the integration of eight Google ct5lp TPUs. This hardware acceleration is bundled directly with the instance to support parallelized tensor computations. However, the server does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options. Additionally, the instance includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of dedicated TPUs relative to the system memory makes this instance highly specialized for machine learning workloads. The main architectural tradeoff is the low system memory per core and the lack of local storage, which requires careful pipeline optimization to prevent data transfer bottlenecks. This server is designed specifically for deep learning training, large-scale AI inference, and other TPU-accelerated workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct5lp-hightpu-8t is a specialized, accelerator-optimized server instance designed for high-compute workloads. It features 224 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 384 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 1.71 GB per vCPU. The instance is equipped with eight bundled Google ct5lp TPUs to handle demanding machine learning tasks. There is no local storage included, requiring reliance on network-attached storage solutions, and the instance provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server offers high accelerator density, making it cost-efficient for specialized machine learning pipelines, though the low system memory per core and lack of local storage represent key architectural tradeoffs. It is best suited for deep learning training, large-scale AI inference, and TPU-accelerated compute workloads.

tagline: An accelerator-optimized instance featuring eight integrated TPUs and two hundred twenty-four dedicated vCPUs for demanding machine learning workloads.

224 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for high-compute processing
384 GB of system memory providing 1.71 GB of RAM per core
